readingQuestions.prompt: 本文を読んで各設問に答えてください。

payload.title: The Secret Lives of Octopuses
paragraphs: Octopuses may be among the strangest intelligent animals on Earth. Unlike us, they carry most of their neurons not in a central brain but spread throughout their eight arms, so that each arm can taste, touch, and even make small decisions on its own. A hungry octopus does not need to wait for orders from a single command centre; one arm can explore a crack in the rocks while the animal's attention is somewhere else entirely.

This unusual body has produced some remarkable talents. In laboratories, octopuses have learned to open jars, solve simple puzzles, and recognise individual human faces. Some have been seen collecting empty coconut shells and carrying them across the sea floor to build a shelter later, a kind of planning once thought to belong only to birds and mammals. They can also change the colour and texture of their skin in an instant, vanishing against a rock or flashing a warning to a rival.

Yet all this cleverness comes with a strange limit. Octopuses live only a year or two, and most die soon after breeding. Because parents never meet their young, almost none of this hard-won knowledge is passed on to the next generation. Every octopus, in a sense, must discover the world from the very beginning, which makes their achievements all the more surprising.

questions.prompt: 本文の主旨として最も適切なものはどれですか。
options: Octopuses are unusually intelligent animals whose strange body and very short lives make their abilities especially remarkable.
Octopuses are simple creatures with no ability to learn or solve any kind of problem.
Octopuses keep all of their neurons in one large central brain, just as humans do.
Octopuses live for many decades and carefully teach their skills to their young.

questions.before: Some octopuses have been seen collecting empty
questions.after: shells and carrying them across the sea floor to build a shelter later.

questions.prompt: タコの神経が体全体に分散していることを示す語句はどれですか。

spans.text: they carry most of their neurons not in a central brain but spread throughout their eight arms
change the colour and texture of their skin in an instant

questions.sentence: This means that much of what one octopus learns simply disappears when it dies.

questions.prompt: 第3段落で筆者が最も伝えたいことはどれですか。
options: Because octopuses live such short lives and never raise their young, their hard-won knowledge is not passed on, making their skills even more impressive.
Octopuses live for a very long time and share everything they learn with their offspring.
Octopuses are born already knowing everything they will ever need to survive.
The short life of an octopus has no effect on how it learns about the world.

readingQuestions.prompt: 本文を読んで各設問に答えてください。

payload.title: The Four-Day Week Experiment
paragraphs: For more than a century, the five-day working week has felt almost like a law of nature. Recently, though, a number of companies around the world have begun to ask a simple question: what would happen if people worked only four days but were paid exactly the same? Several large trials, especially in Britain and Iceland, have tried to find an honest answer.

The early results have surprised many managers. In most of the companies that took part, the amount of work completed stayed the same or even rose slightly. Employees said they felt less tired and more focused, and fewer of them called in sick. With an extra day off each week, many found more time for family, exercise, and rest, and they returned to work with fresh energy.

Of course, a shorter week does not suit every business. Hospitals, shops, and factories that must stay open cannot simply close for an extra day. Still, supporters argue that the experiment challenges an old belief, namely that longer hours always mean better results, and that for many kinds of work the quality of the effort may matter far more than the number of hours spent at a desk.

questions.prompt: 本文が最も伝えたいことはどれですか。
options: Trials of a four-day week suggest that people can stay productive and healthier, though the idea does not fit every kind of job.
The four-day week has failed everywhere it has been tried and should never be considered again.
Every business in the world has already switched to a four-day week with no problems at all.
Working longer hours has been proven to always produce much better results.

questions.before: In most companies that took part, the amount of work completed stayed the same or even

questions.prompt: 4日制がすべての仕事に向くわけではないことを示す語句はどれですか。

spans.text: the five-day working week has felt almost like a law of nature
Employees said they felt less tired and more focused
Hospitals, shops, and factories that must stay open cannot simply close for an extra day
they returned to work with fresh energy

questions.prompt: 本文全体の要点はどれですか。
options: Shorter working weeks can keep output steady and workers healthier, but they cannot be applied to every job.
The number of hours at a desk is the only thing that decides how good someone's work is.
No company has ever tried working fewer than five days a week.
A four-day week is only possible in hospitals and factories.
